title: The Trolley Problem
description: A runaway trolley is heading toward five people. You can divert it to a side track where it will kill one.

sep_citation.title: Moral Theory
sep_citation.quote: In the Trolley Problem, a person can divert a trolley heading for five to a track where it will kill one

title: The Transplant Case
description: A surgeon can save five failing patients by killing one healthy patient and transplanting his organs.

sep_citation.title: Moral Theory
sep_citation.quote: In the Transplant Case, a surgeon can save five failing patients by killing one healthy patient and transplanting his organs

title: Trolley: The Fat Man Variant
description: You can stop the trolley from killing five by pushing a large man off a bridge, killing him instead.

title: Lifeboat Cases
description: Too many people are on a lifeboat with scarce resources, forcing hard choices about who survives.

title: Promise-Breaking and Repaying Debts
description: You promised to return a borrowed weapon, but the owner is not in his right mind and may cause harm.

sep_citation.title: Moral Dilemmas
sep_citation.quote: it would be wrong to repay certain debts—for example, to return a borrowed weapon to a friend who is not in his right mind

title: Prisoner's Dilemma (Moral Variant)
description: Two agents choose between cooperation and defection with outcomes that reward mutual cooperation but tempt betrayal.

title: Murderer at the Door
description: A murderer asks for the location of a potential victim. Telling the truth risks death; lying can protect life.

title: Numbers Rescue Case
description: You can save either one person or five from equivalent serious harm, but not both.
